summary: Problem q2 is a research-level local Rankin-Selberg problem (GL(n+1)xGL(n), per Jacquet-Piatetski-Shapiro-Shalika) requiring construction of a specific Whittaker vector and analysis of the associated local zeta integral, with a low 20% solvability estimate. The single tracked issue is a resolved seed/placeholder that points to no concrete error, meaning no substantive proof currently exists to evaluate. The reported 'best proof' status of 100 open issues against the claude_opus_5-4 model indicates extensive unresolved work and no validated submission.
problems: No actual proof artifact exists yet — the only issue is a closed placeholder seed task with no mathematical content to evaluate.
Core mathematical blocker: constructing a single explicit Whittaker vector W on G whose Rankin-Selberg integral has the required (often p-adically controlled or test-function-prescribed) value.
The 'best proof' carries 100 open issues with an unknown experiment tag, signaling the attempt is far from complete or coherent.
Low solvability (20%) reflects deep prerequisites: Whittaker model theory, Bessel/newform identities, and local zeta integral computations over non-archimedean fields.
Missing specification of the target value/normalization the Whittaker integral must achieve, making the proof goal ambiguous as recorded.
highlights: Problem statement and source are well-attributed (Paul D. Nelson, First_Proof.tex), providing a clear authoritative reference frame.
The placeholder issue was correctly triaged and closed as a non-actionable seed rather than a real defect, keeping the issue tracker clean.
Domain is well-studied (JPSS local theory), so canonical techniques and literature (essential/newform Whittaker vectors, local functional equations) are available to guide construction.

suggested_todos.title: Pin down the exact theorem goal: state precisely which Whittaker vector W must be built and what value/identity its Rankin-Selberg integral must satisfy.
Draft a construction skeleton using the essential/newform Whittaker vector and the JPSS local zeta integral, citing the standard local functional equation.
Triage the 100 open issues on the claude_opus_5-4 best proof: cluster them and identify which block the central Whittaker-vector construction.
Compute the local integral in the unramified/spherical case first as a tractable sub-lemma to validate the approach.
Record the correct experiment tag and clarify the '?' experiment metadata for reproducibility.
